headlines.text: Gunman opens fire at White House Correspondents' Dinner; Trump rushed to safety, suspect in custody
Trump cancels Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan trip, says Iran can 'call us anytime' from their rubble
Iran refuses 'forced negotiations' as US naval blockade redirects 37 ships and counting
Israel strikes Hezbollah in Lebanon again as ceasefire frays at the seams
Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM and Tuareg rebels launch largest coordinated attack in Mali in years, seize Kidal
Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' opens to fans literally dancing in the aisles — and fights about it online
Intel surges 23% on AI-driven earnings blowout, lifting Nasdaq to record close Friday
Gas prices tick up to $4.10 nationwide as Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz in a chokehold
Trump uses WHCA shooting to once again pitch his under-construction White House ballroom as the solution to everything
Pope Leo XIV calls death penalty 'inadmissible' hours after DOJ authorizes firing squads for federal executions

breaking.text: BREAKING: Suspect charged in White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting; arraignment set for Monday, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro says

stories.hed: Trump Tells Envoys to Stay Home, Iran Told to Use a Phone Like Everyone Else
stories.dek: Trump scrapped Witkoff and Kushner's Pakistan trip mid-taxi-to-runway, saying 18-hour flights are for losers and Iran can 'call anytime.' Iran's President Pezeshkian, unmoved, said Tehran won't negotiate under blockade — which is a fancy way of saying the war is very much still on.

stories.hed: Mali Hit by Largest Coordinated Attack in Years; Jihadists and Tuareg Rebels Seize Kidal
stories.dek: Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM and the Azawad Liberation Front launched simultaneous strikes on Bamako, Gao, Sevare, and Kidal in a pre-dawn blitz analysts are calling unprecedented since 2012. The junta leader's whereabouts remain unknown, the defense minister's house was blown up, and the army's 'situation is under control' statement has been met with appropriate skepticism.

stories.hed: Shooting at WHCA Dinner Suspect May Have Targeted Trump Officials, Acting AG Says
stories.dek: A gunman opened fire near the ballroom entrance of the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night, sending journalists and officials diving under tables. One Secret Service agent was shot and is reportedly doing fine. Trump, for his third brush with gun violence, responded by promoting his under-construction White House ballroom.

stories.hed: Pope Leo XIV Condemns Death Penalty as DOJ Greenlit Firing Squads for Federal Executions
stories.dek: Pope Leo XIV called capital punishment 'inadmissible' in a video message released on the same day the Justice Department said it would permit firing squads for federal executions — a coincidence in timing that left very little ambiguity about who was talking to whom.

stories.hed: Trump Turns Assassination Attempt Into a Ballroom Pitch — His Third Time Getting Shot At
stories.dek: For the third time since 2024, Trump found himself near gunfire and responded not with shock but with a Truth Social post about why his White House ballroom would have prevented it. At some point, 'I hate to say I'm honored,' as Trump himself put it, becomes a pretty dark personal brand.

markets.context: Nasdaq and S&P 500 closed at record highs Friday as Intel surged 23% on AI earnings and hopes for US-Iran peace talks briefly lifted sentiment before Trump canceled his envoys' trip.

gas.note: Filling up now costs more than most streaming services combined — and the content is worse.

popCulture.hed: Fans Are Literally Moonwalking in Theater Aisles During the Michael Jackson Biopic 'Michael' — Opening Weekend Goes Feral
popCulture.dek: The long-awaited Michael Jackson biopic starring his nephew Jaafar Jackson opened this week, and concertgoers are rushing to the front of theaters to dance, dividing the internet between 'this is beautiful' and 'I paid $18 to watch your back.' It is already 2026's most chaotic theatrical experience.

genZ.definition: A phrase originally used sincerely to fact-check viral TikToks via the X-based AI chatbot Grok, it has evolved into a sarcastic punchline dropped under any obviously absurd or made-up claim online. In April 2026 it became the go-to way to mock political spin, war coverage takes, and influencer drama without engaging earnestly.
genZ.usage: Trump says the WHCA shooter 'would never have happened' with his White House ballroom. Grok, is this true?

tldr: A gunman at the White House Correspondents' Dinner sent Trump sprinting for the exit and pivoting to ballroom promotion by morning, while across the Atlantic, Mali's capital burned as jihadists and Tuareg rebels launched the country's biggest coordinated offensive in over a decade. Meanwhile, the Iran ceasefire talks collapsed before they even started, gas hit $4.10, and Intel's AI earnings sent the Nasdaq to record highs because the market, like Trump, remains stubbornly unbothered.
